image.shortdescription: a numberline with a shaded region; the shaded region begins at 6 where there is a left bracket; it extends to the right all the way with an arrowhead

solution.p: The interval represents all numbers greater than or equal to 6\text{.} So in set-builder notation, the interval is {\{ x \mid x \ge 6 \}}\text{.}

And we read the number line from left to right, with the shading starting at 6 (and including 6) and extending on to \infty\text{.} So in interval notation, the interval is {\left[6,\infty \right)}\text{.}
